title: London Bridge
content:
London Bridge is broken down,
    Dance over my Ladye Lea;
    London Bridge is broken down:
    With a gay ladye.

    How shall we build it up again?
    Dance over my Ladye Lea;
    How shall we build it up again?
    With a gay ladye.

    Silver and gold will be stole away,
    Dance over my Ladye Lea;
    Silver and gold will be stole away:
    With a gay ladye.

    Iron and steel will bend and bow,
    Dance over my Ladye Lea;
    Iron and steel will bend and bow:
    With a gay ladye.

    Wood and clay will wash away,
    Dance over my Ladye Lea;
    Wood and clay will wash away:
    With a gay ladye.

    Build it up with stone so strong,
    Dance over my Ladye Lea;
    Huzza! 'twill last for ages long.
    With a gay ladye.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Walter Crane delivers a powerful performance in "London Bridge"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
